title: Service Lifetime Management
description: Ollama and the mineflayer adapter both need lifetime managers to make sure they are launched. Ollama also may need some help making sure the right model is running (I think I had to manually call run). MineflayerAdapter manager probably needs a way to see if it has disconnected and needs reconnecting/restarting

comments.author: Agent Smith (Sprint 36)
comments.body:
Kick detection + auto-rejoin gap observed 2026-06-21:

MineflayerAdapter/index.js has NO `bot.on('end', ...)` handler. When the bot gets kicked (e.g. `multiplayer.disconnect.chat_validation_failed` from spamming chat), the adapters's 'kicked' event just logs and forwards to C#, then the process exits with no reconnection.

Layers that need changes:
1. JS (MineflayerAdapter/index.js): Add `bot.on('end', ...)` handler that calls tryReconnect() with exponential backoff. Recreate bot via createBot(). Also handle 'kicked' → end flow properly.
2. C# bridge (WebSocketBridge.cs / MinecraftAdapter.cs): Add reconnect loop on WebSocket drop. Currently ConnectAsync is one-shot.
3. Agent loop (AgentBackgroundService.cs): Handle mid-goal disconnection — restore goal state after reconnect.

Also observed: the 'kicked' event payload contains a NBT compound with a translate key — the server sends `multiplayer.disconnect.chat_validation_failed` when the bot's chat frequency triggers anti-spam. The C# KickedEvent currently stores `e.Reason` as the raw JSON — may need string extraction.
